issues.title: AdvisedRequest.userText is not populated under some ChatClient Configurations.

comments.body:
This is because when constructing Prompt, the order of adding Message is very important, which represents the speaking order of the user and the assistant. When ChatClient constructs a request, if you do not explicitly specify userText, it will get the last Message in the Message list to fill in the userText of this request. 

  In short, in your scenario, you only need to swap the order of userMessage and systemMessage, that is: 

`Prompt prompt = new Prompt(systemMessage, userMessage); `

to solve your problem

issues.title: Method name spelling error
issues.body:
The getToolCallbacksFromAsyncClients method name of the McpToolUtils class is spelled incorrectly

**Bug description**
public static List<ToolCallback> getToolCallbacksFromAsyncClinents(List<McpAsyncClient> asynMcpClients);

**Environment**
1.0.0-SNAPSHOT

**Steps to reproduce**


**Expected behavior**
public static List<ToolCallback> getToolCallbacksFromAsyncClients(List<McpAsyncClient> asynMcpClients);

![Image](https://github.com/user-attachments/assets/ab6fef26-9520-4c33-94e0-be47e0cb2010)

comments.body: This is fixed via bf0558bb7 Thanks @dev-jonghoonpark

issues.title: MCP Client cannot connect to two or more MCP Servers with the same tool names
issues.body:
If an MCP Client is configured with two or more MCP Servers that expose tools with the same name, the client fails to start with and `IllegalStateException: Multiple tools with the same name (doSomethingCool)`.

I understand why Spring AI checks for multiple tools with the same name, but I can see how as MCP becomes more prominent and applications are configured to attach to multiple MCP Servers, the likelihood of this happening are going to increase. And, ultimately it's not the client's fault that there are two servers that have the same name.

Perhaps it would be possible (in the case of MCP) to prepend the tool name with a namespace, either based on the MCP Server's name or even just some UUID or random value. 

FWIW, Claude Desktop doesn't seem to have this issue.  It's unclear to me how they deal with it. Hopefully they do something like namespacing, but I suppose that it's also possible that when a duplicate tool is found at startup, it overwrites the original with the same name (which, if so, would be unfortunate).

comments.body: Followup: As best as I can tell, Claude Desktop doesn't complain when there are two or more MCP Servers with tools that have the same names. But it seems to fixate on only one of those and always use it, even if the prompt tries to steer it to the other one. Which one it chooses seems to be luck of the draw, though...not necessarily the first or last one. And if you start a new chat, it might pick a different MCP Server's tool.

Thanks for catching this @habuma 
I agree we need some sort of prefix in the MCP originated tools.
